front: What is a static variable in Java?
back: A static variable is a class-level variable that is shared by all instances of the class. There is only one copy of a static variable in memory, regardless of how many objects are created from the class.

front: How do you call a static method?
back: A static method is called using the class name, not an instance of the class. For example: `ClassName.staticMethodName();`

front: What is a key restriction on static methods?
back: Static methods cannot directly access instance variables or call non-static (instance) methods. They can only directly access other static members (variables or methods).

front: What is the purpose of a static block?
back: A static block is used to initialize static variables. It is executed only once, when the class is first loaded into memory by the JVM, before any objects are created or static methods are called.

front: Can a class be declared as static?
back: Yes, but only if it is a nested class. A top-level class cannot be declared static. A static nested class can be instantiated without an instance of the outer class.
